_meta.benchmark_name: NONMEM Run 504 — One-Compartment with Covariates (ADVAN1 TRANS2)
_meta.dataset: 501.csv — 60 subjects, 240 IV-infusion observations, covariates: WT, AGE, SEX
_meta.model: 1-compartment IV infusion, power-law covariates on CL and V, BLOCK(2) OMEGA, proportional error
notes: OFV reported without N*log(2pi) constant (NONMEM convention).
OpenPKPD achieves OFV=1139.52 (7.7% worse than NONMEM).
Main discrepancy: V~WT exponent (0.817 vs NONMEM 1.32), CL~AGE exponent (-0.203 vs -0.534).
Likely cause: block OMEGA optimization + covariate interaction creates difficult likelihood surface.
Remedy: better initialization of covariate exponents; tighter bounds on power-law parameters.
_meta.dataset_citation: Bauer RJ (2019). NONMEM tutorial part II: estimation methods and diagnostics for nonlinear mixed-effects models with examples from pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic studies. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol 8(8):538-556. [Dataset 504.csv: 60 subjects, IV infusion, 1-compartment with covariates.]

openpkpd_comparison.diagnosis: Converged but to a suboptimal solution. V_WT_exponent and CL_AGE_exponent differ substantially from NONMEM. Block OMEGA optimization with power-law covariates is challenging.
